notes: Matched configured LangSmith target for app/graph.py.

resolution_notes: Configured LangSmith target aligned with the changed graph artifact.

coverage_by_target.retrieval_notes: Configured LangSmith target aligned with the changed graph artifact.
analysis_notes: Existing baseline cases are factual retrieval checks and do not evaluate citation attribution.

gaps.related_risk_flag: Retrieved context is assembled from raw page_content only, so the model may fabricate or misattribute citations when chunks are ambiguous.
gaps.description: Existing eval coverage does not adequately exercise citation attribution behavior within the discovered judge-style eval method.
gaps.why_gap_is_real: The nearest baseline case checks factual retrieval only and has no rubric about citation presence or attribution accuracy.
gaps.existing_coverage_notes: Current LangSmith cases validate answer correctness, not whether the answer grounds each claim in the right source post.
native_shape_hints: Preserve LangSmith-style dataset examples with a judge rubric in metadata.
Keep the input as a single user query, not a generic probe wrapper.

compatible_nearest_cases.input_normalized: What does Lilian Weng say about types of reward hacking?
compatible_nearest_cases.native_shape_summary: Single-turn LangSmith factual retrieval example with rubric metadata.
compatible_nearest_cases.why_not_sufficient: It checks factual correctness only and does not require source citation.

gaps.related_risk_flag: Cross-post attribution may fail when multiple blog posts are retrieved together.
gaps.description: Existing eval coverage does not adequately exercise citation attribution behavior within the discovered judge-style eval method.
gaps.why_gap_is_real: The nearest multi-topic case still expects one factual answer, not disambiguation across multiple possible source posts.
gaps.existing_coverage_notes: The baseline has no case where the correct answer spans or contrasts multiple blog posts and therefore stresses attribution.
native_shape_hints: Use a question whose answer naturally touches multiple Lilian Weng posts.
Judge on correct attribution, not just topical relevance.

compatible_nearest_cases.input_normalized: How does Lumiere differ from cascade-based video generation approaches like Imagen Video?
compatible_nearest_cases.native_shape_summary: Single-turn comparison question with judge-style grading.
compatible_nearest_cases.why_not_sufficient: It does not require the model to cite or separate multiple candidate sources.

gaps.related_risk_flag: The model may fabricate a source label when the retrieved text does not make the originating post identifiable.
gaps.description: Existing eval coverage does not check whether the model abstains from citing a source when attribution is ambiguous.
gaps.why_gap_is_real: No baseline case asks the model to prefer uncertainty over a fabricated citation.
gaps.existing_coverage_notes: Current cases reward answering, but none test citation restraint under ambiguity.
native_shape_hints: Judge for explicit restraint when the source cannot be identified confidently.
Do not convert this into an exact-match deterministic case.

compatible_nearest_cases.input_normalized: What does the blog say about Mixture-of-Experts architectures?
compatible_nearest_cases.native_shape_summary: Unsupported-question case that expects graceful refusal.
compatible_nearest_cases.why_not_sufficient: It checks unsupported content, not ambiguous citation attribution inside otherwise answerable content.
